target.title: Duplicate command ack comments are posted for a single @clawsweeper issue command
target.body:
## Summary

ClawSweeper posted duplicate command acknowledgement comments for the same issue command on openclaw/openclaw#88102.

## Evidence

On openclaw/openclaw#88102, the issue author posted:

```text
@clawsweeper re-review based on latest comments
```

User command comment:

```text
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88102#issuecomment-4579020705
```

ClawSweeper then posted two separate acknowledgement comments with the same marker:

```text
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88102#issuecomment-4579021116
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88102#issuecomment-4579022447
```

Both bodies started with the same durable marker:

```html
<!-- clawsweeper-command-ack:4579020705 -->
```

The same duplicate behavior happened again for a later author command:

```text
@clawsweeper review based on full details of the ticket plus additional comments
```

User command comment:

```text
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88102#issuecomment-4579054352
```

Duplicate ClawSweeper ack comments:

```text
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88102#issuecomment-4579054896
https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/88102#issuecomment-4579056165
```

Both used the same marker:

```html
<!-- clawsweeper-command-ack:4579054352 -->
```

A screenshot from the issue view also showed two identical `ClawSweeper picked this up. Command router queued. I will update this comment with the next step.` comments under a single user command.

## Expected behavior

For one command comment, ClawSweeper should create or update one acknowledgement comment keyed by `clawsweeper-command-ack:<source-comment-id>`.

If duplicate webhook deliveries, retries, or concurrent routers process the same command, the ack writer should be idempotent and update/reuse the existing ack comment instead of posting a second one.

## Actual behavior

Two acknowledgement comments were created for the same source command comment id.

## Impact

This creates duplicate noise on user-facing OpenClaw issues and makes it harder to tell whether one or two command-router jobs are active.

## Notes

This was observed on openclaw/openclaw#88102 on May 29, 2026. The old issue was later superseded by openclaw/openclaw#88120 for unrelated report-cleanup reasons, but the duplicate ack evidence remains in #88102 comments.
target.author: brokemac79
